title: The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick — Page 715
raw_text:
line for me of remembering him and paradoxically mercifully forgetting him.
But understanding that I can find him in world over and over again, viz: God
discoverable in polyform, but always and only God, however and in what
thing experienced: world deconstructed into God always. Thus I am pried
away from transitory manifestations which do disappear and am instead
bonded to the eternal; but I find it in world and as world, not in withdrawal
from world. Thus there is a double motion: pried loose from that which fails;
bonded to that which is discoverable always, always capable of being
renewed. Again found, unlike people and things seen in themselves: discrete
particulars.
work_title: The Exegesis of Philip K. Dick
